book.title: Trust Before Intelligence
book.author: Ram Katamaraja
book.core_thesis: Enterprise AI failure stems from infrastructure gaps, not AI limitations. 95% of enterprise generative AI pilots fail. Organizational trust must precede technical sophistication.

INPACT.description: Six diagnostic dimensions agents need from organizational infrastructure

dimensions.N: Natural — Business language understanding without schema knowledge
dimensions.T: Transparent — Observable reasoning, complete audit trails

INPACT.scoring: 1-6 per dimension, summed to 36, converted to 100-point scale

GOALS.description: Five operational excellence pillars for sustaining trustworthy AI systems

pillars.G: Governance — Security, compliance, ABAC authorization, HITL workflows
pillars.O: Observability — Monitoring, cost tracking, distributed tracing, transparency
pillars.A: Availability — Speed, scale, data freshness, semantic caching
pillars.L: Lexicon — Semantic understanding, disambiguation, 1000+ business terms

seven_layer_architecture.description: Technical infrastructure blueprint fulfilling INPACT needs

layers.1: Multi-Modal Storage — RDBMS, NoSQL, Graph, Object, Lakehouse, Model Registry
layers.2: Real-Time Data Fabric — CDC (Debezium), Event Streaming (Kafka), Stream Processing (Flink)
layers.3: Semantic Layer — Glossary (1000+ terms), Entity Resolution, Ontologies, Knowledge Graphs
layers.4: Intelligence Layer — 7-stage RAG pipeline (query understanding → caching)
layers.5: Governance — ABAC engine (OPA), HITL workflows, policy versioning
layers.6: Observability — Distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry), APM, cost attribution, drift detection
layers.7: Orchestration — Multi-agent coordination, context management, HITL escalation

causes.1: Data Foundation Gaps (30%) — BI-era batch ETL incompatible with agent sub-2s needs
causes.2: Architecture Misalignment (25%) — Role-based access vs required context-aware ABAC
causes.3: Demo-Driven Development (20%) — POC mentality, no operational infrastructure
causes.4: Reinvention vs Framework (15%) — Custom solutions instead of systematic approach
causes.5: Conceptual Misunderstanding (10%) — Treating agents as advanced search vs autonomous systems

trust_band.description: Readiness scoring system for AI deployment

bands.86-100: High Trust — Production-ready, minimal gaps (2-4 weeks)
bands.67-85: Moderate Trust — Pilot-ready with known gaps (4-8 weeks)
bands.50-66: Low Trust — Significant work required (8-12 weeks)
bands.33-49: Very Low Trust — Major transformation needed (12-16 weeks)

sections.mapped_concept: Five Root Causes of AI Failure
sections.learning_goal: Diagnose your organization's AI readiness by applying the Five Root Causes of AI Failure framework (Data Gaps, Architecture Misalignment, Demo-Driven Development, Reinvention, Misunderstanding) and establishing a Trust Band baseline score.

sections.mapped_chapter: Chapter 0: Trust Before Intelligence / Chapter 2: INPACT Framework
sections.learning_goal: Design a trust architecture using the INPACT framework (Instant, Natural, Permitted, Adaptive, Contextual, Transparent) to define the six infrastructure dimensions that must precede AI deployment.

sections.mapped_chapter: Chapter 1: Why 95% of Agents Fail (Root Cause #3)
sections.learning_goal: Evaluate and score AI opportunities against INPACT Permitted and Transparent dimensions to separate responsible initiatives from demo-driven development traps.
Map AI capabilities to your industry's value chain using the GOALS Lexicon pillar (semantic understanding, entity resolution) and 7-Layer Architecture Semantic Layer patterns.
Synthesize INPACT diagnostic, GOALS operational pillars, and Phase Progression frameworks into a unified AI strategy with a baseline Trust Band score and Phase 1 Foundation plan.

sections.mapped_chapter: Chapter 1: Why 95% of Agents Fail (all five causes)
sections.mapped_concept: Five Root Causes of AI Failure (complete taxonomy)
sections.learning_goal: Classify the full spectrum of AI risks using the Five Root Causes taxonomy (Data Gaps 30%, Architecture Misalignment 25%, Demo-Driven 20%, Reinvention 15%, Misunderstanding 10%) and prioritize by organizational exposure.

sections.mapped_concept: GOALS Solid pillar — data quality & integrity
sections.learning_goal: Design data classification policies aligned with GOALS Solid pillar requirements (accuracy 98%, completeness 99.5%, consistency 95%) and 7-Layer Architecture Layer 1 Multi-Modal Storage patterns.
Design layered guardrails mapped to 7-Layer Architecture Layer 6 Observability (distributed tracing, APM, alerting) and GOALS Observability pillar for failure detection and recovery.

Design HITL oversight patterns using 7-Layer Architecture Layer 5 Governance (5-factor ABAC authorization, policy evaluation <10ms) with INPACT Permitted dimension compliance targeting <15% escalation rate.
Build a governance framework integrating all five GOALS pillars (Governance, Observability, Availability, Lexicon, Solid) with Trust Band target scoring to validate operational readiness.

sections.section_title: Translating Business Goals into System Requirements
sections.learning_goal: Translate business goals into measurable system requirements using INPACT Instant (<2s response, <30s freshness) and Natural (entity resolution >98%) performance specifications.
Design data pipelines using 7-Layer Architecture Layers 1-2 (Multi-Modal Storage with 11 modal types + Real-Time Data Fabric with CDC and event streaming) for sub-30s data freshness.

Model decision flows using 7-Layer Architecture Layer 4 Intelligence (7-stage RAG: query understanding → embedding → retrieval → reranking → context assembly → generation → caching) with INPACT audit requirements.

sections.mapped_concept: 7-Layer Architecture Layer 6 Observability + Root Cause #1 mitigation
secondary_frameworks: Five Root Causes (#1 Data Foundation Gaps)
sections.learning_goal: Design edge case recovery patterns using 7-Layer Architecture Layer 6 Observability (trace completeness >98%, automated alerting) and Five Root Causes #1 Data Foundation Gaps mitigation strategies.
Produce a complete AI requirements specification scored against INPACT composite dimensions and mapped to Phase Progression Phase 1-2 deliverables (Foundation + Intelligence infrastructure).
Design the Planner-Builder-Reviewer pattern mapped to 7-Layer Architecture Layer 7 Orchestration (multi-agent coordination, shared state management, HITL escalation routing).

sections.mapped_concept: 7-Layer Architecture Layer 7 + Five Root Causes (#4 Reinvention)
secondary_frameworks: Five Root Causes (#4 Reinvention vs Framework)
sections.learning_goal: Define planning-execution boundaries using Phase Progression methodology to prevent Five Root Causes #4 Reinvention vs Framework failure — systematic approach over custom solutions.
Break complex AI tasks into atomic, verifiable steps with INPACT Instant (<2s) verification checkpoints and 7-Layer Layer 7 state management (Redis, 15-min TTL) for progress tracking.
Design quality gates using 7-Layer Layer 6 Observability (distributed tracing, 15-25 spans/query) and GOALS Observability pillar with Trust Band evidence requirements at each gate.
Integrate 3-agent system into a controlled build methodology targeting Phase Progression Phases 1-3 completion with Trust Band improvement from baseline to Moderate Trust (67+).

sections.mapped_concept: Trust Band Scoring as credibility instrument
sections.learning_goal: Map stakeholders and build coalition support using Trust Band Scoring (0-100) as a credibility instrument and Five Root Causes as a structured objection-handling framework.

sections.mapped_concept: GOALS Governance & Observability pillars — board-ready translation
sections.learning_goal: Translate GOALS Governance and Observability pillars into board-ready language with INPACT Transparent dimension compliance evidence (audit trails, trace completeness, cost attribution).

sections.mapped_concept: Phase Progression ROI model — Echo Health case study
sections.learning_goal: Build a multi-dimensional ROI model using Phase Progression Echo Health benchmark ($942K investment, 209% Year 1 ROI, 10-week payback) as the reference framework.

sections.mapped_chapter: Chapter 12: Running Agents at Scale / Chapter 10 (Phases 3-4)
sections.learning_goal: Design the Phase 3-4 scaling path (Trust → Operations) using the full 7-Layer Architecture stack with a production Trust Band target of High Trust (86+).

sections.mapped_concept: Phase Progression (complete: Foundation → Intelligence → Trust → Operations)
sections.learning_goal: Synthesize all program frameworks into a 90-day roadmap following the Phase Progression model (Foundation Wk 1-4 → Intelligence Wk 5-7 → Trust Wk 8-10 → Operations Wk 11-12) with INPACT milestone targets per phase.
